title: Old-Fashioned Onion Soup
description: This recipe requires only four ingredients (onions, butter, French bread, and chicken broth), but you need to start simmering this soup ten to eighteen hours before you plan to serve it.
ingredients: 7 slices French or Italian bread, cut into 1 inch cubes
instructions: Place sliced onions and butter into slow cooker, and mix until onions are thoroughly coated. Stir in bread and chicken broth.
Cover, and cook on LOW for 10 to 18 hours or on HIGH 4 to 5 hours, stirring occasionally. Stir well during last hour.
author: [{'@type': 'Person', 'name': 'CORWYNN DARKHOLME'}]

featured_tweaks.text: First off. For anyone substituting beef broth, that is French Onion. This is Traditional Onion. There are plenty of French Onion recipes on this site, try one of those, not this. That being said, this is the BEST onion soup! I scaled the recipe down because it’s just me and my hubby. 3 lbs of onion is a lot for two. So, I used 2 Vidalia Onions and 2 - 32oz chicken stock. I added 2 cloves of garlic and 4 skinless boneless chicken thighs (cubed). I imagine the recipe, as is, could be a little bland. So, as suggested I added my favorite spices. I used a dash of garlic powder, oregano, basil, rubbed sage, smoked paprika and chipotle chili powder. I slow cooked on low for 8 hours. I added the bread last, after it was cooked and on top of the soup then topped with gruyere cheese. I broiled it till the cheese was nice and bubbly. I was too scared to cook the bread the whole time in the crockpot. This recipes will be a staple in my house from now on. Thank you for this recipe, it is a must try recipe.

Liked doing this but in Crock pot. Usually just stovetop. I added Mushrooms, a bit less bread and as a Vegetarian I used Vegetable broth. 
Put cheese and crotons on top after coming from the Crockpot and Baked it a bit. Awesome
Yum. I didn't put in the bread (we served it on the side). I also needed much more liquid! Next time I'll either use small onions, or chop the rings in half as it was a bit messy to eat.
Used Chicken Stock instead of Broth (Costco), tore up the slices of bread. I think next time I'll quarter the slices of onion. It was a little messy to eat.

This is a good starter recipe. Next time I make it I will use a bit less onion andquite a bit more broth. The changes I already made were to use beef broth instead of chicken, and I didn't add the bread while it cooked. I added the bread to the bowls and put provolone cheese on top. Delicious!

I love love love having dinner ready for me. My boyfriend said this was the best onion soup recipe I've made yet. I used Vidalia onions and cut the recipe in half. The only change I made was to use a really good beef broth instead of chicken. The soup was good as is, but of course I couldn't resist adding another slice of bread topped with cheese, baked on top. Excellent comfort food on what turned out to be a very difficult day for us both. Thanks for the submission.

this was great! I made the soup in my slow cooker but did not put the bread or cheese in it until I was ready to serve it. I put the bread and cheese on the soup and tosted it in the oven... very yummy

This soup requires excellent onions to turn out as savory and flavorful as needed. If you're working with lower-quality or even run-of-the-mill onions, you will need to season it. I added white wine to pump up the flavor, as well as a bay leaf.
This recipe was very easy. I added a little sherry and mixed chicken and beef broth. I also toasted my bread with olive oil.
